message:
test(mt-mvcc-bench): use prepared statements on fsqlite side (apples-to-apples)

The rusqlite reference workload (L413-415) prepares the INSERT once per
transaction and binds (id, payload) as parameters via rusqlite::params![]
for every row. The fsqlite workload was doing the opposite — building a
distinct SQL string per iteration via format!("INSERT INTO bench (id,
payload) VALUES ({id}, 'tid{tid}_i{i}')") and calling conn.execute(&sql)
on each one. Because the SQL text was unique per call, fsqlite's parse
cache was never hitting; every insert paid the full lex+parse+plan cost.

This was visible in the 2026-04-23 post-T3b perf capture as:
- `Lexer::tokenize_into`  2.53% self-time (SQL tokenization on every op)
- allocator cluster (_int_malloc + cfree + malloc + malloc_consolidate)
  totalling ~13% self-time — mostly AST/plan/row-value churn

Rewrite the fsqlite loop to match rusqlite's pattern:

    let stmt = conn.prepare("INSERT INTO bench (id, payload) VALUES (?1, ?2)")?;
    for i in 0..rows_per_thread {
        let params = [
            fsqlite::SqliteValue::Integer(id),
            fsqlite::SqliteValue::Text(payload.into()),
        ];
        stmt.execute_with_params(&params)?;
    }

Post-fix measurement (2026-04-23 19:41 UTC, ts2):

| Threads | fs_wps pre-fix | fs_wps post-fix | Δ      |
|--------:|---------------:|----------------:|:-------|
| 1       | (not measured) |          85,604 | —      |
| 2       | (not measured) |           8,773 | —      |
| 4       |          5,943 |           5,991 | ~same  |
| 8       |          5,067 |           5,043 | ~same  |

The 1t jump to 85,604 wps (vs the previous apples-to-oranges 16,533 wps at
1t) is fsqlite's true single-thread INSERT throughput with prepared
statements. The 2-thread 10x cliff is now clearly exposed as an MVCC
contention point — post-fix perf shows two new hotspots that were masked
by the parsing overhead:

- `fsqlite_vdbe::engine::wait_for_page_lock_holder_change`  2.94%
- `InProcessPageLockTable::unregister_waiter`               2.80%

These are the threads spinning on another writer's page lock. That is
the real structural bottleneck the campaign needs to address next.

Evidence:
- /data/tmp/fsqlite-t3b-validation-185110/perf.data (pre-fix)
- /data/tmp/fsqlite-bench-fix-validation-194151/perf.data (post-fix)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
